title: cerastium arvense var. bracteatum
intro: Cerastium arvense var. bracteatum, also known as Sticky Mouse-ear Chickweed, is a perennial plant species within the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that belongs to the genus Cerastium. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cerastium arvense var. bracteatum are attributed to its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, salves, and tinctures that can be applied topically to alleviate skin conditions and promote overall health.
